message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Desert)
Cuisine/type signal: Jewish delicatessen and bakery
Why it surfaced in discovery: Coachella Valley's signature Jewish deli, established 1963 by Sherman Harris; the Palm Desert location produces baked goods for both desert outposts. Family-owned, now run by Harris's children Sam and Janet.
Discovery sources: https://shermansdeli.com/ | https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/shermans-deli-palm-springs/ | https://visitpalmsprings.com/3-longest-operating-restaurants-in-palm-springs/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Information Extract

## Locations & Addresses
- **Palm Springs**: 401 E. Tahquitz Canyon Way, 760-325-1199
- **Palm Desert**: 73161 Country Club Drive, 760-568-1350

## Hours
"We're open daily 8am to 9pm" with early dining specials from 4pm-6pm year-round (extended to 7pm July-October)

## History & Ownership
Established in 1963, Sherman's operates as "a New York-style family restaurant still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet."

## Menu Structure
The establishment offers breakfast, brunch, lunch, and dinner service featuring:
- Fresh-baked items (cinnamon bread French toast, daily breads/desserts)
- Sandwich board with 40+ options
- Dinner entrées (rotisserie chicken, brisket, BBQ ribs, fried chicken)
- Dinners include soup/salad, sides, and vegetables

Additional offerings: wines, beer, and Sherman's Original Smokey & Tangy BBQ Sauce ($7.95)

## Contact Information
- Phone: 760-325-1199 (Palm Springs) or 760-568-1350 (Palm Desert)
- [Online ordering available](https://shermansdeli.com/order-online/)
- Social: Facebook and Instagram (@shermansdeli)

## Structured Data
No JSON-LD or microdata markup detected in provided HTML source.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli & Bakery in Palm Desert:

## Overview

Sherman's Deli & Bakery is Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery, family-owned and operated. Established in 1963, Sherman's has become a landmark in the desert and is still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet.

## Yelp Reviews

On Yelp, Sherman's is described as "Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery." The restaurant has a strong presence on Yelp with significant review volume and maintains a Health Score of 96 out of 100.

## Google/TripAdvisor Reviews

The reviews are mixed:
- **Positive**: Reviewers praise the "fantastic pastrami sandwich" that is "loaded with meat," with "great service" and "fair prices."
- **Mixed**: Some customers found "the food was unremarkable and overpriced" and "the baked goods terrible."
- **Pricing Concerns**: Some long-time customers note "their prices keep going up" to the point where they won't return, citing costs like ">$50 for lunch for two for two sandwiches and no drinks."

## Notable Features

Fieri raved about Sherman's delicious rye bread, up to 100 loaves a day baked in-house. The restaurant is also very dog friendly with an outside patio.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli in Palm Desert and its appearance on Guy Fieri's show:

## Sherman's Deli on Diners, Drive-Ins & Dives

Guy Fieri visited Sherman's Deli and Bakery in Palm Desert for his Food Network show Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives. Fieri discovered the restaurant while in the desert for an "awesome concert."

### What They Serve

Sherman's delivered full deli nostalgia for Guy Fieri, including turkey pastrami, house-made rye bread, and a latke brisket sandwich that leaned hard into classic Jewish deli tradition. This landmark eatery has been dishing out East Coast flavor and hospitality since the 1950s.

### The Show's Impact

After the show aired, the turkey pastrami became the most popular sandwich on the menu, catapulting the number of turkey breasts prepared each week from 10 to 30. Guy praised Sherman's as a "gem" and "outstanding."

### Location Information

Sherman's has two locations in the Coachella Valley: 73161 Country Club Drive, Palm Desert, CA (760) 568-1350 and 401 East Tahquitz Canyon Way, Palm Springs, (760) 325-1199.

input.prompt: Extract total review count, rating, ranking among Palm Desert restaurants, and recent review sentiment from 2025-2026.

content.content:
# Sherman's Deli - Palm Desert Summary

**Review Count:** 1,672 reviews

**Rating:** 4.4 of 5 bubbles

**Restaurant Ranking:** #1 of 12 Dessert Spots in Palm Desert

**Recent Review Sentiment (2025-2026):**

Sherman's Deli receives mixed feedback from recent visitors. Positive reviews highlight excellent breakfast items, with one guest noting "Corned beef hash under poached eggs beside giant breakfast potatoes. The flavour was wonderful." Another visitor praised the pastrami sandwich, stating it "almost melts in your mouth."

However, negative reviews cite concerns about service inconsistency and pricing. One recent complaint mentioned that "my Chicken Sesame Salad included over cooked rubbery chicken," while another diner noted ">$50 for lunch for two for two sandwiches" is "exorbitant." Service quality varies, with some praising attentive staff and others describing inattentive service during busy periods.

Overall, the restaurant maintains its reputation for quality deli offerings but faces criticism regarding operational consistency and value proposition post-pandemic.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast restaurants in Palm Desert:

## Top Breakfast Restaurants in Palm Desert

Some of the top breakfast restaurants in Palm Desert include Wilma & Frieda's Cafe, Restaurante Los Primos, Breakfast Republic, Beautiful Day, Broken Yolk Cafe, and Paradise Cove Cafe.

### Popular Choices:

**Paradise Cove Cafe** - Hidden between Trader Joe's and Michael's is Paradise Cove Café, an overwhelming favorite of the residents. Paradise Cove Café is known for its creative menu, outstanding service, and world-famous pancakes. The restaurant features a legendary Palm Springs Mimosa, made with a scoop of Blood Orange Sorbet.

**Wilma & Frieda's Cafe** - Named after the owner's two grandmothers, Wilma & Frieda serves up some of the best comfort food in the Coachella Valley with two locations in the Greater Palm Springs area.

**Restaurante Los Primos** - A favorite in Palm Desert with customers who discover it and return regularly.

**Keedy's Fountain & Grill** - A storied, old-school diner in Palm Desert that has been a favorite with locals since the 1950s, offering all-day breakfast, burgers, and classic milkshakes.

**Louise's Pantry** - A cherished La Quinta establishment that has been serving up classic comfort food and nostalgic charm since 1946, offering a trip back in time with its old-fashioned ambiance and hearty, homestyle cooking.

Based on the search results, here are the best Jewish delis in the Coachella Valley, Palm Springs, and Palm Desert areas:

## Top Jewish Delis:

**Sherman's Deli & Bakery**
Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery, established in 1963. It's the only kosher-style deli with a bakery in Palm Springs. They have two locations: one in Palm Springs and one in Palm Desert.

**Manhattan in the Desert**
A genuine New York-style Jewish deli specializing in lox, homemade soups, hot dogs and sandwiches, the latter of which are all served "mile-high," stacked to perfection and with a free sampling of delicious pickled goods. The deli also boasts a sister location in Palm Desert (which was featured on "Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives!"), but the original is the best.

**Deli Chick**
Deli Chick has the best sandwiches in the Coachella area with a clean, casual and friendly atmosphere.

**Wexler's Deli**
Located at the Arrive Hotel in Palm Springs, Wexler's has been serving up classic Jewish deli food offerings, including house-smoked and hand-sliced meats and fish since opening in the fall of 2018.

**J's Deli**
Located in Palm Desert around the corner from El Paseo, this new Deli is large and clean with a variety of dishes for all times of the year.

Here are the search results for Sherman's Deli in Palm Desert on Country Club Drive:

**Location & Hours:**
Sherman's Deli & Bakery is located at 73161 Country Club Dr, Palm Desert, CA 92260 and is open 8:00 am - 9:00 pm daily.

**Breakfast:**
Breakfast features fresh baked cinnamon bread French toast and all American breakfasts like huge 8-egg omelets with thick bacon. Bread and desserts are baked fresh daily.

**About the Restaurant:**
Sherman's is Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery. Family-owned and operated, their goal is to make you feel at home.

**Phone & Contact:**
You can reach them at (760) 568-1350.

input.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
input.cuisine_or_type: Jewish delicatessen and bakery (kosher-style, New York-style)
input.corridor: north Palm Desert / Country Club Drive corridor

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~2,369 reviews (Palm Desert location); Tripadvisor ~1,672 reviews; combined with Palm Springs sibling location pushes the brand to 6,000+ reviews across platforms
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive with pricing-driven mixed undercurrent
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025-2026) reviews praise breakfast (corned beef hash, cinnamon French toast), pastrami, and house-baked rye, but a recurring criticism centers on rising prices ('$50 for two sandwiches'), occasional service inconsistency, and post-pandemic value perception.
reputation.longevity: Established 1963 by Sherman Harris; 60+ years operating; still family-owned and run by Harris's children Sam and Janet — ownership stability confirmed across multiple sources
credentials: Featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives with Guy Fieri (Palm Desert location)
KESQ Best of Coachella Valley — Best Desserts winner
Listed among Visit Palm Springs' '3 longest-operating restaurants'
Palm Desert Area Chamber of Commerce member

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best Jewish deli Palm Springs/Palm Desert/Coachella Valley', 'Diners Drive-Ins and Dives Palm Springs', 'pastrami sandwich Coachella Valley', and 'longest-operating restaurants Palm Springs'. Does NOT surface in generic 'best breakfast Palm Desert' lists (Wilma & Frieda's, Paradise Cove, Keedy's dominate) despite serving full breakfast daily — a notable gap given their cinnamon-bread French toast and 8-egg omelets. Does not appear in 'best bakery Palm Desert' agent answers despite baking up to 100 loaves of rye daily.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When it surfaces, descriptions are generally accurate (Jewish deli, family-owned, 1963, two CV locations). Some sources slightly conflate the Palm Desert vs Palm Springs locations or misstate founding decade ('1950s' in one DDD recap) — minor but worth noting.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: shermansdeli.com has clean location/hours/phone content and a functional menu structure, but NO JSON-LD or schema.org microdata detected. No Restaurant, LocalBusiness, Menu, or PostalAddress schema. Hours and addresses are human-readable only. Online ordering routes through NetWaiter (third-party). This is a meaningful agent-readability gap for a 60-year landmark brand.

competitors: Manhattan in the Desert (Palm Desert) — closest direct Jewish-deli competitor, also DDD-featured
Wexler's Deli (Palm Springs, Arrive Hotel) — modern boutique Jewish deli
Keedy's Fountain & Grill (Palm Desert) — old-school diner competing on breakfast/longevity
J's Deli (Palm Desert, near El Paseo) — newer entrant in same category
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — for north Palm Desert / Country Club Drive corridor and for the Coachella Valley Jewish-deli category broadly. Serves as the bakery production hub for both Sherman's outposts, making it operationally central to the brand.
query_wins: Diners Drive-Ins and Dives Palm Desert
pastrami sandwich Palm Springs / Palm Desert

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — family-owned, second-generation (Sam Harris and Janet Harris), two-location independent operation, no franchise/chain/resort affiliation
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns shermansdeli.com (real site, multi-page, dedicated breakfast/menu/locations/online-order pages); active Facebook and Instagram (@shermansdeli); paid/featured listings in Palm Springs Life directory and Visit Greater Palm Springs tourism portal; Chamber of Commerce member; sells branded retail BBQ sauce. Evidence of ongoing budget but no apparent SEO/schema agency footprint.
convertibility.reasoning: Sherman's is a textbook convertible: a 60-year independent landmark with reachable second-generation owners (Sam and Janet Harris), an existing real website, active social presence, and demonstrated willingness to spend on directory listings and branded merchandise. The current visibility gap is concrete and fixable — they win category-specific 'Jewish deli' queries but miss broader 'best breakfast Palm Desert' and 'best bakery Palm Desert' surfaces where their actual product (in-house baking, full breakfast, 60-year story) would justify inclusion. Absent schema.org markup is the most actionable lever. Two-location footprint amortizes any agency or schema work across both addresses.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Google Maps review count — could not directly access Google reviews; Yelp Palm Desert volume (~2,369) and Tripadvisor (~1,672) verified, Google count inferred to be in the 1,000-3,000 band but not directly confirmed
[UNVERIFIED] Specific KESQ Best of Coachella Valley award year — search surfaced the win for 'Best Desserts' but year not confirmed

[UNVERIFIED] The 'Restaurant of the Year' and 'No. 1 Sandwich in the Desert' recognitions attributed to California State Senate/Assembly, Riverside County Supervisors, and Palm Springs Chamber appeared in one search snippet but could not be independently confirmed on a primary source — treat with caution

[UNVERIFIED] One source claimed the deli has been operating 'since the 1950s' which conflicts with the 1963 founding stated on shermansdeli.com — 1963 is the authoritative date from the owner's own site
Yelp Palm Desert page returned 403 to WebFetch; review count taken from search-result snippet rather than direct page read
